name: Form Configuration Best Practices
summary: Guidelines and best practices for generating high-quality JSON Forms configuration

layoutSelection.description: Choose the right root layout based on form complexity

checks: All UI schema scopes reference properties that exist in the data schema
Required fields in data schema are clearly marked in the `required` array
Each Control has a valid scope starting with `#/properties/`
No orphaned required fields (every required field has a matching property)
Data schema `type` is `"object"` at the root level

rules: Include descriptive labels — property names are auto-converted to labels but may need refinement
Add inline help for complex fields using `options.help`
Use HelpContent elements for important notices, privacy statements, or instructions
Provide clear error messages in validation rules
Keep help text at grade 9 reading level for citizen-facing forms

commonFields.description: Use ADSP common schema definitions instead of building fields from scratch

fields.name: Full Name
fields.renders: First name, middle name, and last name input fields

fields.name: Address
fields.renders: Structured postal address (addressLine1, municipality, subdivisionCode, postalCode)

commonFields.usage: Use schemaDefinitionTool to load definitions, then reference them with $ref in data schema properties

iterativeWorkflow.description: How to build forms iteratively with the user
steps: Load existing form configuration with formConfigurationRetrievalTool
Ask for the purpose of the form if unclear
Start with 1-3 fields and apply via formConfigurationUpdateTool
Ask for user feedback after each update
Add more fields, refine layouts, and add help text incrementally
Don't dump JSON — describe changes and use the update tool
